title: Christella parasitica
intro: Christella parasitica, also known as the parasitic fern or resurrection fern, is a type of epiphytic fern that grows on trees in tropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain and improving overall well-being. Bioactive constituents of Christella parasitica include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Christella parasitica is often prepared as a tea or extract, and is also used in traditional remedies to treat various conditions, including fever, cough, and skin irritations.
taxonomy: Christella parasitica is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Christella, which is a type of wood fern. Christella parasitica is further classified as a member of the order Polypodiales and class Polypodiopsida. The species is native to Asia and is known to inhabit tropical and subtropical regions. Christella parasitica is also known to thrive in shaded, humid environments.

These active constituents are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties, which include antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities.
